title: heliconia laufao
intro: Heliconia laufao, also known as 'Puka' in some Pacific Island cultures, is a flowering plant native to Fiji, Samoa, and Tonga. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, such as poultices or salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
